tasks.name: Choose the cheapest monthly plan under $40 that supports at least 3 chatbots with analytics
tasks.description: Select a pricing plan that costs less than or equal to $40 per month, allows managing at least 3 separate chatbots, and explicitly includes conversation analytics, then start the signup flow for that plan.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the "Pricing" link in the main navigation menu.
Scroll down to view all available monthly plan cards listed on the pricing page.
Identify all plans that show a monthly price of $40 or less and list "3 chatbots" or more in their feature list.
From those qualifying plans, look for the ones whose feature list explicitly includes "Conversation analytics" or similar wording.
Among the remaining qualifying plans, determine which has the lowest monthly price shown on its card.
Click the primary action button (for example, "Get Started" or "Choose Plan") on the cheapest qualifying plan’s card.

On the plan signup form that appears, type any company name into the "Company name" field, enter "user@example.com" into the email field, choose any password in the password field, and click the button to create the account for that selected plan.

tasks.name: Create a new customer support chatbot for an online store using an e-commerce template
tasks.description: Use the templates gallery to create a new chatbot named "Store Support Bot" based on a template labeled for e-commerce customer support, and set its primary language and greeting message.
steps: From the logged-in dashboard, click on the "Chatbots" or "Bots" item in the main sidebar.
Click the "Create new chatbot" or "+ New Bot" button on the chatbots list page.
In the creation dialog or page, click the "Browse templates" or "Choose from templates" option.
In the templates gallery, use the category or tag filters to select templates related to "Customer Support" and "E-commerce" or "Online Store".
Among the filtered templates, select the first template that includes both "Support" and "E-commerce" (or similar) tags in its description or label.
Click the "Use this template" or equivalent button on that template card.
On the chatbot setup form, type "Store Support Bot" into the chatbot name field and select "English" from the primary language dropdown.
In the greeting or welcome message field, enter the text "Hi! I can help you with orders, shipping, and returns today." and click the "Save" or "Create chatbot" button to finalize this new chatbot.

tasks.name: Add three FAQ entries to the chatbot knowledge base for shipping, returns, and order status
tasks.description: Configure the knowledge base for an existing chatbot by adding exactly three FAQ articles: one for shipping time, one for return policy, and one for order status checks, and assign each to the appropriate category.
steps: In the dashboard sidebar, click on "Chatbots" and then click on the name of an existing chatbot to open its settings.
Within that chatbot’s settings, click the "Knowledge Base" or "FAQs" tab.
Click the "Add FAQ" or "+ New Article" button to create the first entry.

In the first FAQ form, enter the question "How long does shipping take?" and the answer "Standard shipping takes 3–5 business days within the US." then select or create the category "Shipping" and save the FAQ.
Click the "Add FAQ" or "+ New Article" button again to create the second entry.

In the second FAQ form, enter the question "What is your return policy?" and the answer "You can return unused items within 30 days for a full refund." then assign the category "Returns" and save the FAQ.
Click the "Add FAQ" or "+ New Article" button a third time to create the final entry.

In the third FAQ form, enter the question "How can I check my order status?" and the answer "You can track your order using the tracking link sent after purchase or by providing your order ID in the chat." then choose the category "Orders" and save the FAQ.

tasks.name: Customize the chat widget’s appearance with a specific color and position
tasks.description: Adjust the chatbot widget so it uses a custom brand color, appears in the bottom-right corner, and shows a specific header title before saving the appearance settings.
steps: From the dashboard, click on "Chatbots" and select an existing chatbot to configure.
In the selected chatbot’s settings, click on the "Appearance" or "Widget" tab.
Locate the color customization section and click the color input or color picker for the widget’s main accent color.
Enter the hex code "#1A73E8" into the color value field to set the widget accent color and confirm the selection.
Find the "Position" or "Placement" setting and select the option labeled "Bottom right" or the equivalent bottom-right placement choice.
In the header title or widget title field, type "Need help with your order?" as the new title text.
Scroll down if necessary and toggle on any setting that enables showing the title in the widget header (for example, a switch labeled "Show title"), if it is not already on.
Click the "Save changes" or "Save appearance" button to apply the new color, position, and header title settings.

tasks.name: Set up business hours routing so daytime chats go to the Sales team and others stay with the bot
tasks.description: Create a routing rule that sends chats received Monday through Friday between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM to a "Sales" team, while keeping all other times handled by the bot only, then enable the rule.
steps: In the dashboard sidebar, click on the "Routing" or "Automation" section.
On the routing rules page, click the "Add rule" or "+ New Rule" button.
In the rule name field, type "Weekday Sales Hours".
Find and click the time-based condition control (for example, "Add condition" then choose "Business hours").
Set the days of the week to Monday through Friday by selecting the checkboxes or options for "Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", and "Fri".
Set the start time for the business hours condition to 9:00 AM and the end time to 5:00 PM.
In the action section of the rule, choose the option that routes conversations to a team and select "Sales" from the team dropdown list for conversations that match the business hours condition.

For the fallback or "otherwise" behavior of this rule, select the option that keeps conversations handled by the bot only (for example, "Bot handles conversation" or no agent assignment), and then click "Save" or "Enable" to activate the routing rule.

tasks.name: Enable and configure a lead capture flow that collects name, email, and company
tasks.description: Turn on lead capture in the chatbot and configure a short form that asks for full name (required), email (required), and company (optional), ensuring the fields appear in that order in the conversation.
steps: From the dashboard, click on "Chatbots" and choose the chatbot where you want to capture leads.
Within that chatbot’s settings, click the "Lead Capture" or "Forms" tab.
Toggle on or enable the lead capture feature if it is currently disabled.
Click the "Add field" or "+ Field" button and create a text field labeled "Full Name"; set its requirement setting to "Required".
Click the "Add field" or "+ Field" button again to create an email field labeled "Email"; set this field as "Required" as well.
Click the "Add field" or "+ Field" button a third time to create a text field labeled "Company"; set this field as "Optional".
Use the drag handles or ordering controls to arrange the fields so that "Full Name" is first, "Email" is second, and "Company" is third in the list.
Click the "Save" or "Save lead form" button to apply the lead capture configuration to the chatbot.

tasks.name: Review analytics to find the day last month with the highest average messages per conversation
tasks.description: Filter conversation analytics to the previous calendar month, sort by average messages per conversation per day, and open the details for the day with the highest average.
steps: In the dashboard navigation, click on the "Analytics" or "Reports" section.
On the analytics page, locate the date range picker and open it by clicking on it.
Select the option or manually set the date range to cover the entire previous calendar month (for example, from the 1st to the last day of that month).
Apply the date range filter by clicking the "Apply" or "Update" button.
In the analytics view, switch to the report or tab that shows daily metrics, including "Average messages per conversation" for each day.
Locate the table or chart that lists each day as a separate row or item and use the sorting controls to sort the days by "Average messages per conversation" in descending order (highest first).
Identify the top entry in the sorted list, representing the day with the highest average messages per conversation.
Click on that top day’s row or data point to open the detailed view or breakdown for that specific day.

tasks.name: Add two team members with different roles and assign one to a chatbot
tasks.description: Create two new team member accounts, assigning one the "Admin" role and the other the "Editor" (or similar lower) role, then assign the Editor to manage a specific chatbot.
steps: From the main dashboard menu, click on the "Team" or "Users" section.
On the team management page, click the "Invite member" or "+ Add User" button.

In the invitation or user creation form, enter "alex.admin@example.com" into the email field, type "Alex Admin" in the name field, and select the "Admin" role from the role dropdown before clicking the button to add this user.
Click the "Invite member" or "+ Add User" button again to add a second user.

In the second form, enter "emma.editor@example.com" into the email field, type "Emma Editor" in the name field, and select the "Editor" or the next lower permission role from the role dropdown before clicking the button to add this user.
After both users are listed, navigate to the "Chatbots" section using the sidebar.
Click on the name of a specific existing chatbot to open its settings or access configuration.

Within that chatbot’s settings, find the "Members", "Collaborators", or "Permissions" tab, click it, and then use the add member control to add "Emma Editor" to this chatbot, granting her access to manage it, and save the changes.

entities.name: PricingPlan
entities.description: Subscription plan shown on the Pricing page and used during plan signup.

fields.name: max_chatbots
fields.description: Number of chatbots allowed on this plan

fields.name: feature_list
fields.description: Array of feature description strings shown on the plan card

entities.name: Subscription
entities.description: Represents the single account’s active subscription to a pricing plan, created via the Plan Signup form.

entities.name: Chatbot
entities.description: A chatbot configuration instance managed by the single account.

fields.name: template_id
fields.description: References ChatbotTemplate.id when created from a template

entities.name: ChatbotTemplate
entities.description: Pre-built templates used to create new chatbots, filterable by categories/tags like Customer Support and E-commerce.

fields.name: categories
fields.description: Array of category strings such as 'Customer Support', 'E-commerce'

entities.name: KnowledgeBaseCategory
entities.description: Category for chatbot FAQ/knowledge base entries, e.g., Shipping, Returns, Orders.

entities.name: KnowledgeBaseArticle
entities.description: FAQ entry / knowledge base article for a chatbot.

entities.name: ChatbotAppearance
entities.description: Widget appearance settings for a chatbot (color, position, header title).

entities.name: LeadCaptureForm
entities.description: Lead capture configuration attached to a chatbot.

entities.name: LeadCaptureField
entities.description: Field definition within a lead capture form (e.g., Full Name, Email, Company).

entities.name: Lead
entities.description: Lead captured by a chatbot’s lead capture flow.

entities.name: Team
entities.description: Team or department such as 'Sales' used for routing conversations.

entities.name: RoutingRule
entities.description: Automation rule defining when chats are routed to a team vs handled by the bot.

fields.name: days_of_week
fields.description: Array of day-of-week values such as 'mon','tue','wed','thu','fri','sat','sun'

fields.name: start_time
fields.description: Start time in HH:MM 24h or 12h-normalized format, e.g., '09:00'

fields.name: end_time
fields.description: End time in HH:MM format, e.g., '17:00'

fields.name: match_team_id
fields.description: References Team.id when match_action_type is route_to_team

fields.name: fallback_team_id
fields.description: References Team.id when fallback_action_type is route_to_team

entities.name: TeamMember
entities.description: Member of the account’s team with a role like Admin or Editor.

entities.name: ChatbotMember
entities.description: Assignment of a team member as a collaborator on a specific chatbot.

entities.name: DailyAnalyticsSummary
entities.description: Pre-aggregated daily analytics used in the Analytics & Reports section.

fields.name: chatbot_id
fields.description: Optional: aggregate for a specific chatbot; null means all chatbots
